L1_headline: + PEN – Corporate governance filing with anti-takeover provisions. | 02/25/26
L2_brief: Penumbra filed corporate governance details showing common stock traded on NYSE under 'PEN' with 300 million authorized shares. The company maintains anti-takeover provisions including staggered board terms and Delaware Section 203 protections, though board terms will transition to one-year structure by 2028.

risk_factors: Authorized preferred stock may facilitate anti-takeover defense
Staggered board increases time to change board composition
Delaware Section 203 restricts business combinations with 15%+ stockholders
Various provisions make acquisition of control more difficult
Preferred stock issuance may adversely affect common stockholder rights

ma_and_deal_relevance.acquisition_activity: Anti-takeover provisions designed to discourage coercive takeover practices and encourage negotiation with board

legal_and_regulatory: Subject to Delaware Section 203 anti-takeover statute
key_developments: Board structure transitioning from staggered three-year terms to one-year terms starting 2026
Full transition to declassified board by 2028
Seven-member board within authorized range of 5-9 directors
